metadata.title: Neoantigen Vaccine Pipeline — Engineering Immunity to Your Own Cancer's Mutations

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Sawubona, Liam — in for Bear. Neoantigen vaccines — engineering immunity to your own cancer's mutations? Can you explain it, Bear?

props.command: Neoantigen vaccines — engineering immunity to your own cancer's mutations?

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Neoantigen vaccines — engineering immunity to your own cancer's mutations.

Every tumor carries mutations the immune system could learn to recognize — if it were taught. The mRNA-4157 melanoma trial cut recurrence by 44 percent in combination with pembrolizumab. Can this scale beyond melanoma and beyond affluent clinical trial sites?

Research personalized neoantigen cancer vaccines: mRNA-4157 trial results in melanoma, pipeline steps, manufacturing timeline and cost, extension to other tumor types, and main barriers to broad adoption.

props.command: claude "Research personalized neoantigen cancer vaccines: the mRNA-4157/V940 Moderna-Merck trial results (recurrence-free survival in stage III/IV melanoma), the pipeline steps (tumor sequencing -> neoantigen prediction -> mRNA manufacturing -> dosing), manufacturing timeline and cost, extension to other tumor types (NSCLC, colorectal), and the main barriers to broad adoption."

beats.narration_text: Python script tracing pipeline stages with timeline and cost estimates per stage.

props.code:
STAGES = [
    {"stage": "Tumor biopsy + QC",        "days": 3,   "cost_usd": 500,   "attrition_pct": 10},
    {"stage": "Whole-genome sequencing",   "days": 14,  "cost_usd": 1500,  "attrition_pct": 5},
    {"stage": "Neoantigen prediction",     "days": 7,   "cost_usd": 3000,  "attrition_pct": 15},
    {"stage": "mRNA manufacturing",        "days": 28,  "cost_usd": 50000, "attrition_pct": 8},
    {"stage": "QC release + dosing",       "days": 7,   "cost_usd": 2000,  "attrition_pct": 2},
]
cumulative_loss = 0
print(f"{'Stage':<30} {'Days':>5}  {'Cost':>8}  {'Loss':>6}  {'Cumulative loss'}")
print("-" * 70)
for s in STAGES:
    cumulative_loss = 100 - (100 - cumulative_loss) * (1 - s["attrition_pct"]/100)
    print(f"{s['stage']:<30} {s['days']:>5}d  ${s['cost_usd']:>7,}  {s['attrition_pct']:>5}%  {cumulative_loss:>8.1f}%")

beats.narration_text: Pipeline stages output — tumor biopsy, whole-genome sequencing, neoantigen prediction, mRNA synthesis, dosing — with timeline and estimated cost per stage.
Add the main failure point at each pipeline stage — what percentage of patients fail to reach dosing due to biopsy quality, sequencing turnaround, prediction accuracy, or manufacturing failure?

props.command: claude "Add the main failure point at each pipeline stage — what percentage of patients fail to reach dosing due to tumor biopsy quality, sequencing turnaround, neoantigen prediction accuracy, or manufacturing failure?"

beats.narration_text: Revised pipeline with attrition rates at each stage — cumulative loss from biopsy to dose.

The vaccine works when it reaches the patient. The attrition from biopsy to dose is the real barrier — not the biology. A pipeline that fails 30 percent of patients before they receive a single injection is a manufacturing problem, not a science problem. Solving scale is the next five years of this field.

Read the V940 trial preprint on survival outcomes and find the subgroup analysis by tumor mutation burden. High-TMB patients should respond better — verify whether the effect size is larger in that group, and whether the trial was powered to show it.
Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI. Then take it apart. At Nik Bear Brown, nikbearbrown dot com.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Neoantigen Vaccine Pipeline — Engineering Immunity to Your Own Cancer'] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
